description_long: This is a vintage, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and elliptical wings, painted in a brown and orange camouflage pattern with red star insignias on each wing. The aircraft features a single engine mounted at the nose, with a three-blade propeller and a forward-positioned, enclosed cockpit canopy. The wings are low-mounted and straight, with rounded tips and visible ailerons. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both with rounded edges. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel, all visible in the images. The surface finish appears matte with some weathering, and the overall design suggests a World War II-era fighter aircraft.
description: A vintage, single-propeller airplane with brown-orange camouflage, red star insignias, and a streamlined, low-wing design. Features an enclosed cockpit, fixed landing gear, and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the elliptical wingspan, three-blade propeller, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the low-mounted wing, red star insignia, and the streamlined fuselage profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite wing, propeller, and the tail wheel, mirroring the left side's features.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight elliptical wings with rounded tips and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers, all with rounded edges.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy integrated into the fuselage.
